description: §59 / §14.10 (Rx-6) — a bare `.Variant` in the VALUE position of a map method on a REACTIVE map cell resolves against the map's declared value type. `<m>: [string:Color]` then `@m = @m.insert("a", .Red)` and `@m.getOr("a", .Blue)` must compile CLEAN — before the fix the reactive-cell-map path lost the map's value-type context for bare-variant inference and false-fired E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS on `.Red` / `.Blue`. (The non-reactive LOCAL map read path was already clean; the real repro is the reactive-map value arg.) A qualified `Color.Red` and a typo (`.Rd` → E-TYPE-063) both behave correctly.
rationale: §59.2/§59.4: a map is `[KeyT: ValT]`; §14.10 supplies the value-type context for a bare variant at a fixed-type position. The VALUE arg of `insert(k, v)` / `getOr(k, default)` is a value of the map's value type, so a bare `.Variant` there resolves against that enum — no E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS.
